help.text:
HTTP/1.1 PROTOCOL REFERENCE
════════════════════════════════════════════════

REQUEST FORMAT:
  METHOD PATH HTTP/1.1\r\n
  Header: Value\r\n
  Header: Value\r\n
  \r\n                          ← blank line ends headers
  [optional body]

COMMON METHODS:
  GET      Retrieve a resource
  POST     Create a resource (body required)
  PUT      Replace a resource (body required)
  PATCH    Partial update (body required)
  DELETE   Remove a resource
  HEAD     Like GET but response has no body
  OPTIONS  Show allowed methods for a resource

RESPONSE STATUS CODES:
  1xx  Informational    100 Continue
  2xx  Success          200 OK, 201 Created, 204 No Content
  3xx  Redirection      301 Moved Permanently, 302 Found, 304 Not Modified
  4xx  Client Error     400 Bad Request, 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden,
                        404 Not Found, 405 Method Not Allowed, 429 Too Many
  5xx  Server Error     500 Internal Server Error, 502 Bad Gateway,
                        503 Service Unavailable

ESSENTIAL HEADERS:
  Host: hostname           (REQUIRED in HTTP/1.1)
  Content-Type: mime/type  (for POST/PUT/PATCH)
  Content-Length: N        (byte count of body)
  Accept: mime/type        (preferred response format)
  Authorization: scheme credentials
  Connection: close|keep-alive
  User-Agent: product/version
  Cookie: name=value

COMMON CONTENT TYPES:
  application/json                  JSON data
  application/x-www-form-urlencoded Form data
  text/html                         HTML
  text/plain                        Plain text
  multipart/form-data               File uploads

HTTPBIN.ORG TEST ENDPOINTS:
  /get         Echo GET request data
  /post        Echo POST request data
  /put         Echo PUT data
  /delete      Echo DELETE data
  /headers     Show request headers
  /ip          Show origin IP
  /user-agent  Show User-Agent
  /status/:N   Return HTTP status N
  /redirect/:N Redirect N times
  /basic-auth/:user/:pass  HTTP Basic auth test
  /cookies     Show cookies
  /delay/:N    Delay response by N seconds

Socwrap HTTP Macros:
  //get //post //put //delete //head //options //patch  Request lines
  //host //useragent //close //keepalive                 Headers
  //accept-json //accept-html //accept-any               Accept headers
  //content-json //content-form //content-length          Content headers
  //auth-basic //auth-bearer //cookie                    Auth/cookie
  //end                                                  End headers (blank line)
  //demo-get  //demo-post  //demo-headers                Demos

Usage:  socwrap --macros --macro-file http-protocol.json --crlf -t HOST PORT

end.description: End headers — send blank line to trigger response

steps.display: Target: httpbin.org/get (echoes request details back as JSON)
Step 6: End headers with blank line  (macro: //end)
═══ Demo complete — server response should appear above ═══
The JSON response echoes back your request headers, origin IP, and URL.

demo-post.description: Demo: POST request with JSON body to httpbin.org/post

steps.display: Target: httpbin.org/post (echoes POST data back as JSON)
═══ Demo complete — server echoes your JSON in the 'data' field ═══

demo-headers.description: Demo: Inspect request headers via httpbin.org/headers

steps.display: Target: httpbin.org/headers (returns all received headers as JSON)
Step 5: Add a custom header (X-Socwrap: testing)
═══ Demo complete — response shows all headers the server received ═══
Notice X-Socwrap appears in the response — any header you send is visible.
